data.title: Here's What to Expect from Nvidia's (NVDA) Upcoming Developer Event
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) will hold its annual GTC conference from March 16 to 19, and the stakes are high for the artificial intelligence (AI) chipmaker.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analyst expect Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang and other senior executives to use the annual developer event to boost confidence in AI scalability and reinforce the company's networking leadership. This will involve Nvidia showcasing multiple new hardware products, including a possible new chip for inference, which is the process of generating results from AI models.

Another key component to Nvidia's upcoming conference will be for the company to demonstrate "AI capex durability," according to Swiss bank UBS (UBS). This means reassuring investors that the $660 billion being spent on AI this year by hyperscalers such as Alphabet (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) is sustainable.

Nvidia's Challenges

The big challenge facing Nvidia is for the company to keep up with global demand for its microchips and processors that power AI applications and models. To that end, many analysts on Wall Street want to see assurances at the GTC conference that shipments of Nvidia's next-generation Vera Rubin chips and hardware are on track and that orders are being filled as forecast.

"We expect comments around market sizing & growth rates, along with product introductions, to be a modest positive for the stock," wrote Truist Securities in a note to clients about the GTC event. NVDA stock could use a boost from the upcoming conference. The company's shares have been stuck around $185 since last August.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 39 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 38 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NVDA price target of $273.61 implies 49.07% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Bitcoin Rises Toward $72,000 as Bessent Greenlights Russian Oil at Sea to Save Economy
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is climbing back toward $72,000 this morning after a surprise move from the U.S. Treasury helped calm a nervous market. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced late Thursday that the government is giving a green light for countries to buy Russian oil that is already at sea. This decision helped pull oil prices down from their recent peaks, giving investors the confidence to jump back into riskier assets like Bitcoin.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Treasury Creates a New Plan to Move Oil at Sea

The new "General License 134" allows for the sale of Russian crude oil and petroleum products that were already loaded onto ships by March 12. This temporary authorization will stay in place until April 11. By letting this "stranded" oil hit the market, the government is trying to stop energy prices from spiraling out of control. Secretary Bessent explained the move on X, stating that "To increase the global reach of existing supply, the U.S. Treasury is providing a temporary authorization to permit countries to purchase Russian oil currently stranded at sea."

Oil Prices Pull Back as Fears Ease

Before this news broke, oil prices had jumped nearly 10% to hit almost $100 per barrel. This spike was caused by the ongoing war in the Middle East and the closure of major shipping routes like the Strait of Hormuz. After Bessent's announcement, crude oil prices dropped by about $2 per barrel. Bessent reassured the public that the current price jump is a "short-term and temporary disruption" that will eventually lead to a "massive benefit" for the U.S. economy in the long run.

Bitcoin Reclaims Its Ground

As oil prices fell, Bitcoin quickly moved up by 2.2%, trading just shy of the $72,000 mark. The coin has shown a lot of strength by holding above $70,000 even during the worst of the energy price scare. Traders are starting to see Bitcoin as a way to avoid the risks associated with traditional markets that are tied too closely to oil and war news. While the stock market has struggled lately, the "digital gold" is finding its own path upward.

At the time of writing, Bitcoin is sitting at $71,502.76.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Acurx Pharmaceuticals Stock (ACXP) Explodes 218% as New Trial Moves Forward on 96% Cure Rate
data.description:
Acurx Pharmaceuticals (ACXP) is having a record-breaking week after the company announced a major update for its lead drug, ibezapolstat. The stock has exploded by over 218% in just the last five days, with most of that jump happening after a massive Monday announcement. Investors are piling in because the company's new clinical trial could completely change how doctors treat and prevent dangerous gut infections.

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Inching Higher Today, 3/12/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were modestly higher on Thursday night as investors waited for key U.S. inflation data, the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) report. However, overall sentiment remained cautious as the Iran war continued to weigh on markets despite a slight pullback in oil prices.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were up 0.21%, 0.44%, and 0.34%, respectively, at 8:30 p.m. EST on March 12.

Crude prices have eased slightly but remain elevated. At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was modestly lower but still trading above $100 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate fell about 0.5% to around $95 per barrel.

Meanwhile, major indices fell sharply on Thursday's regular trading session as oil prices jumped on renewed worries about supply disruptions. The Nasdaq, the Dow, and the S&P 500 dropped 1.8%, 1.6%, and 1.5%, respectively.

The fall came after Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, stated that the Strait of Hormuz should remain closed to pressure enemies.

Coming to the PCE report, consensus expects headline PCE to rise 0.3% month over month and 2.9% from a year ago. Core PCE is projected to increase 0.4% for the month and 3.1% annually.

Further, Q4 GDP (First Revision) will also be released tomorrow. Markets are looking for a revision to the 1.5% growth rate. In addition, the JOLTS Job Openings report on Friday will provide a deeper look into labor demand (job vacancies) for January.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Gold Prices Are Currently Stuck in a Deadlock as Oil Surges and Inflation Fears Rise
data.description:
Gold (CM:XAUUSD) is currently stuck in a deadlock after a two-day slide. The metal is trading near $5,100 an ounce as investors weigh two powerful forces. On one side, oil is climbing due to the war in the Middle East. On the other side, the U.S. dollar is strong, interest rates are high, and inflation fears are rising. For gold, this is like being caught between a rock and a hard place, making it difficult for the price to find a clear path.

data.title: Elon Musk Is Close to Becoming the World's First Trillionaire as His Net Worth Hits $839 Billion
data.description:
Elon Musk remains the world's richest person with an estimated net worth of $839 billion, according to Forbes magazine.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The annual ranking of the world's richest people calculates that Musk's net worth rose by $500 billion in 2025 despite ongoing troubles and a stagnant stock price at electric vehicle maker Tesla (TSLA). At his current trajectory, Musk is on track to achieve a $1 trillion net worth this year, especially once his commercial space company SpaceX holds its initial public offering (IPO) in coming months.

Musk is already the first person ever to reach a net worth greater than $800 billion, according to Forbes, which sees the technology entrepreneur becoming the world's first trillionaire by the end of 2026. While much of Musk's net worth comes from his ownership of Tesla, the SpaceX IPO is expected to be the biggest ever with the company's valuation pegged at $1.75 trillion.

The World's Other Billionaires

Musk's net worth dwarfs the other billionaires on Forbes' 2026 wealth list. In fact Musk's net worth is nearly $600 billion more than the second place person on this year's list, Larry Page, cofounder of Google (GOOGL), whose net worth stands at an estimated $257 billion.

The rest of the top five richest people are also technology entrepreneurs, and include Google cofounder Sergey Brin at number three with a net worth of $237 billion. Amazon's (AMZN) Jeff Bezos is at number four with $224 billion, and Meta Platforms (META) CEO Mark Zuckerberg rounds out the top five with a net worth of $222 billion.

Is TSLA Stock a Buy?

Tesla's stock has a consensus Hold rating among 31 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 13 Buy, 11 Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average TSLA price target of $399.25 implies 0.42% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why the SPY ETF Plunged Today, 3/12/2026?
data.description:
The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) tanked 1.52% today, March 12, due to intensified fears over oil supply disruptions caused by the escalating Iran conflict. Surging oil prices raised concerns about stagflation and reduced the likelihood of Fed rate cuts, prompting a broad risk-off selloff.

data.title: Stock Market Today Review: SPY, QQQ Tumble as Oil Approaches $100 on Iranian Leader's Vow to Keep Hormuz Closed
data.description:
It was another rough day for the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ), as both ETFs closed down by over 1.5% on Thursday as the U.S.-Iran war dragged on into its 13th day.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • In his first publicly broadcast message as Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei said that the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed as a "tool to pressure the enemy" and vowed to strike U.S. bases in the Middle East. Crude oil futures (CL) surged 10% to $95 following his comments.
  • The Trump administration announced trade investigations into 16 countries, including China, the European Union, Mexico, and India, under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974. The investigation centers on excess capacity and production and could lead to new tariffs and fines.
  • U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer noted that the U.S. would announce additional Section 301 trade investigations against 60 countries as soon as Thursday.
  • The U.S. trade deficit fell by 25.3% to $54.5 billion as exports jumped to a record high of $302.1 billion.
  • Microsoft (MSFT) announced a partnership with AMD (AMD) to develop its next-generation Xbox console.
  • Morgan Stanley (MS) closed in the red after the firm joined a growing number of financial firms to limit withdrawals from one of its private credit funds.
  • Palantir (PLTR) finished higher after announcing new collaborations with Nvidia (NVDA), GE Aerospace (GE), and Ondas (ONDS) at its AIPCon 9 event.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Energy was the best-performing sector for a second consecutive day amid rising oil prices and the risk of a prolonged closure to the Strait of Hormuz. Higher oil prices typically boost profits for oil producers, driving gains in stocks like Occidental Petroleum (OXY) and Chevron (CVX) as investors anticipate stronger earnings.

Several energy stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Occidental Petroleum (OXY)
  • Phillips 66 (PSX)
  • Chevron (CVX)
  • Marathon Petroleum (MPC)
  • Valero Energy (VLO)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, industrial stocks posted the worst performance on Thursday. With higher gas prices, transportation and travel companies face increased operating costs, weighing on profits and margins. The sector also struggled as investors assessed the impact of supply chain challenges and global trade uncertainty.

Notable industrial stocks trading lower include:

  • Southwest Airlines (LUV)
  • Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL)
  • GE Aerospace (GE)
  • United Rentals (URI)
  • Emerson Electric (EMR)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Flat as Investors Eye PCE Inflation Report
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were flat on Thursday evening as investors looked forward to the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) report, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.07%, 0.13%, and 0.12%, respectively, at 6:38 p.m. EDT on March 12.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Major indices fell sharply on Thursday's regular trading session as oil prices jumped on renewed worries about supply disruptions. The Nasdaq, the Dow, and the S&P 500 dropped 1.8%, 1.6%, and 1.5%, respectively.

The fall came after Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, stated the Strait of Hormuz should remain closed to pressure enemies. Following this, global benchmark Brent crude surged over 9% to settle at $100.46 per barrel, its first close above $100 since 2022.

Coming to the PCE report, consensus expects headline PCE to rise 0.3% month over month and 2.9% from a year ago. Core PCE is projected to increase 0.4% for the month and 3.1% annually.

Further, Q4 GDP (First Revision) will also be released tomorrow. Markets are looking for a revision to the 1.5% growth rate. In addition, the JOLTS Job Openings report on Friday will provide a deeper look into labor demand (job vacancies) for January.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: "Buy Before the Breakout": Here's Why Top Mizuho Analyst Is Bullish on Micron Stock (MU) ahead of Q2 Earnings
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) is getting another bullish review ahead of its fiscal Q2 earnings report, due on March 18. Top Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh reiterated his Buy rating and price target of $480 (18.4% upside potential), citing powerful tailwinds in the memory market and accelerating AI‑driven demand.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Strong Setup Heading into Earnings

Rakesh sees meaningful upside for Micron as hyperscaler spending continues to surge. He noted that 2026 capex for major cloud players is now expected to jump 59% year-over-year, creating a favorable backdrop for memory pricing and supply tightness.

The analyst's estimates are well above the consensus. He expects May‑quarter revenue of $25 billion and EPS of $11.13, about 8% higher than Wall Street estimates. For the August quarter, he projects $27.2 billion in revenue and $12.25 EPS, roughly 7% above expectations.

Memory Pricing and AI Demand Are Key Drivers

Rakesh pointed to several factors driving Micron's momentum, including strong double‑digit quarterly price gains in both DRAM and NAND at a time when new capacity remains limited.

He also sees solid demand for HBM3e ahead of next year's HBM4 rollout, which is expected to bring 30%-45% higher pricing. Micron is also benefiting from rising LPDDR5 content, with Rubin‑generation AI systems using three times more memory than Nvidia's (NVDA) Blackwell.

On the NAND side, AI‑driven adoption of QLC eSSDs and upcoming 3YY/4XXL transitions through 2026–27 add further support.

Overall, the analyst believes that Micron is set to benefit from one of the strongest memory cycles in years, thanks to growing demand from AI servers, HBM, and next‑generation data center designs.

Is Micron a Good Stock to Buy?

Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Micron stock based on 26 Buys and one Hold recommendation. The average MU stock price target of $440.67 indicates 8.71% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi
